primary_error.message: this invariant could not be proved to be maintained by the loop
primary_error.context: invariant forall k :: 0 <= k < |res| ==> exists i :: 0 <= i < j && s[i] == res[k] && (forall m :: 0 <= m < i ==> s[m] != res[k])

repair_strategy.problem_summary: The postconditions and loop invariants were incorrectly specified, and there was a syntax error in variable initialization.

problem_details.what: Multiple issues: (1) Postconditions were overly complex and included contradictory logic; (2) Loop invariant 'invariant |res| == |res|' was a tautology and useless; (3) Variable initialization used 'var res: string := []' instead of 'var res := ""'; (4) The invariant tried to ensure uniqueness which was not required.
problem_details.why: The postconditions included unnecessary complexity like checking for first occurrence and a contradictory condition '(exists cToRemove :: cToRemove in charsToRemove && v[i] == cToRemove) ==> false'. The loop invariant failed because it tried to prove properties about character uniqueness that weren't necessary. The initialization syntax was incorrect for strings in Dafny.
problem_details.how: Simplified the postconditions to only ensure: (1) result length is bounded by input length, and (2) all characters in result exist in input and are not in charsToRemove. Fixed variable initialization to use empty string. Replaced the useless tautology invariant with 'invariant |res| <= j' to track growth. Simplified the loop invariant to match the simplified postcondition.
